name: rig
description: rig — a quality-gated AI workbench for Claude Code. `/rig:rig "<task>"` classifies the task, shows why (a routing banner), composes the right harness, runs it in an isolated git worktree (patterns/isolated-worktree + scripts/workbench.py), checks it against explicit acceptance-gate criteria (standard + bugfix/feature/refactor/review/security presets, resolving to passed/passed_with_warnings/failed/pending/skipped), and only lands the diff on `accept` — whose accept_requirements checklist (worktree_exists/base_branch_recorded/diff_summary_generated) can't be bypassed even with --force. `/rig:rig board` gives a single dashboard of every active task — run several tasks at once via `/rig:queue go --provider rig` (each dispatched through the same isolation, so parallel headless processes never collide) and never lose track across terminals. `/rig:rig cockpit` goes further: a single read-only Mission Control screen combining run timeline, gate radar, drill-measured reviewer confidence, cost meter, and a force-bypass safety strip, so multi-task status never requires more than one command. `/rig:rig install-git-hook` extends the gate's mechanical secret-pattern sensor to plain `git commit`/`git push` too, opt-in and never silently overwriting a hook it didn't install. Visual-verification screenshots (patterns/visual-artifacts) live under `.rig/` and are pruned by `workbench.py gc` (age-based) or immediately on `discard` — disposable evidence, not the deliverable. `/rig:rig stats` aggregates past runs and flags reviewers with zero rejects as possible rubber stamps. Also a LEGO-style harness composer: compose bricks (facets/patterns/steps/recipes) at invocation time into a task-specific agent harness. Domain-agnostic engine: dev-flow orchestration (review, implement, PR), pluggable packs — sales deal-review, a conversational mode (rig:talk), a goal-driven loop (rig:goal), existing-PR review (rig:pr), GitHub Issue/PR/CI workflows (rig:rig gh — Issue/PR bodies and review comments are quarantine-scanned for prompt-injection with the same catalog `/rig:import` uses, before they ever reach a worktree), a reviewer-detection-rate drill (rig:drill) scoring detection/severity/blocking/explanation accuracy, and a prose AI-smell remover (de-ai-smell) — plus a --validate doctor, a /rig:init scaffolder, generators for reviewer personas (/rig:persona) and LLM-wiki domain knowledge (/rig:knowledge), self-extension that authors new bricks/packs from a description and validates them (/rig:forge), a /rig:catalog cross-tier registry, and run-continuity that survives context compaction via a shipped PreCompact hook. `scripts/mcp_server.py` exposes the same workbench/orchestrate operations over a stdlib-only MCP stdio server so an external agent, CI, or another process can drive rig without a live Claude Code session — opt-in, no new engine, accept/discard safety unchanged. Tiered (project/global) personas and a cross-linked knowledge wiki keep domain knowledge shared, not siloed. Claude Code native; no heavy DSL engine.
